name: ALP dark matter (haloscope-targeted)
summary: Generic axion-like particles (ALPs) — pseudo-scalars with coupling g_a-gamma to two photons, not necessarily the QCD axion — produced via misalignment and constituting the dark matter. Distinct from the QCD axion in that the mass and photon coupling are independent parameters. The string axiverse generically predicts a tower of ALPs spanning many decades in mass; haloscope experiments (ADMX, HAYSTAC, ABRACADABRA, DMRadio) target specific (m_a, g_agamma) windows.

addresses_puzzles.note: Misalignment-produced ALPs naturally saturate Omega_DM h^2 ~ 0.12 across a wide (m_a, f_a) range. Distinguished from QCD axion by lack of the m_a ~ Lambda_QCD^2 / f_a relation; m_a and g_agamma are independent free parameters covering a vast parameter space.

touches_benchmarks.note: ADMX, HAYSTAC, ABRACADABRA, DMRadio, BREAD all sensitive to specific (m_a, g_agamma) regions, providing complementary haloscope coverage from neV to meV ALP masses.

ALP DM oscillation at frequency m_a/(2 pi) drives oscillating fundamental constants; precision atomic-clock comparisons (Sr/Rb, Hg+/Yb+) sensitive to ultralight-ALP (m_a < 10^-15 eV) coupling at competitive levels.

introduces.note: ALP mass and photon coupling; relic abundance is set by initial misalignment angle theta_initial (often counted as a third parameter).

history: Misalignment mechanism: Preskill-Wise-Wilczek (1983), Abbott-Sikivie (1983), Dine-Fischler (1983). ALP generalisation beyond QCD axion is implicit in those works. Modern haloscope program: ADMX since 1996, HAYSTAC 2017, ABRACADABRA, DMRadio. Theoretical motivation from string axiverse (Arvanitaki-Dimopoulos-Dubovsky-Kaloper-March-Russell 2009, arXiv:0905.4720) sharpened the case for non-QCD ALPs.

references.citation: Preskill, Wise, Wilczek, 'Cosmology of the invisible axion', Phys. Lett. B 120, 127 (1983)
Sikivie, 'Experimental tests of the invisible axion', Phys. Rev. Lett. 51, 1415 (1983)
